description: Convert all E2E test cases to it.concurrent for parallel execution within files. Eliminate shared mutable state between tests. Split files when faucet budget exceeds 99 XRP.

userStories.title: Convert validation, wallet, and offline test files to it.concurrent
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all no-network and filesystem-isolated test cases to run concurrently so that these fast test files finish even faster.
acceptanceCriteria: Change every `it(` to `it.concurrent(` in ALL validation test files: tests/e2e/*/**.validation.test.ts (check, channel, clawback, credential, deposit-preauth, escrow, multisig, nft, oracle)

Change every `it(` to `it.concurrent(` in ALL wallet test files: tests/e2e/wallet/*.test.ts (address, alias, change-password, decrypt-keystore, fund, import, list, new, new-mnemonic, private-key, public-key, remove, sign, verify)
Change every `it(` to `it.concurrent(` in tests/e2e/channel/channel.sign.test.ts (offline crypto, no shared state)
No beforeAll or test logic changes needed — isolation already exists (validation: no network; wallet: mkdtempSync per test; sign: pure crypto)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is purely mechanical: s/^  it(/  it.concurrent(/ in each file. Wallet tests already create a fresh mkdtempSync tmpDir inside each test body. Validation tests have no shared state. channel.sign.test.ts is pure offline crypto.

userStories.title: Convert simple network test files to it.concurrent
userStories.description: As a developer, I want small network test files that already use createFunded per test converted to it.concurrent with recalculated ticket budgets.
acceptanceCriteria: Change every `it(` to `it.concurrent(` in: tests/e2e/payment/payment.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/payment/payment.tokens.test.ts, tests/e2e/clawback/clawback.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/ticket/ticket.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/multisig/multisig.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/account/account.set.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/account/account.keys.test.ts, tests/e2e/account/account.query.test.ts
For each file: verify each it.concurrent creates its own wallets via createFunded inside the test body — no shared wallet variables consumed across tests
For each file: recalculate TICKET_COUNT = total wallets funded across ALL tests (concurrent means all run at once, all tickets consumed simultaneously)
For each file: add budget comment above TICKET_COUNT: `// N tests × W wallets × A XRP + T tickets × 0.2 = X ≤ 99 ✓`
For each file: verify budget constraint: TICKET_COUNT × 0.2 + total_funded_XRP ≤ 99 XRP; if exceeded split into two files each with own beforeAll
Set timeout on each it.concurrent that funds wallets inside the body: { timeout: 90_000 }
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Most of these files already call createFunded per test. The main work is: (1) change it( to it.concurrent(, (2) recalculate TICKET_COUNT since all tests now run simultaneously, (3) verify budget. client and master are read-only after beforeAll — safe to share.

userStories.title: Convert medium network test files to it.concurrent
userStories.description: As a developer, I want medium-complexity network test files converted to it.concurrent with any shared wallet state moved into individual test bodies.
acceptanceCriteria: Change every `it(` to `it.concurrent(` in: tests/e2e/oracle/oracle.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/deposit-preauth/deposit-preauth.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/nft/nft.core.test.ts, tests/e2e/trust/trust.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/offer/offer.network.test.ts
For each file: remove any file-level `let` variables holding wallets or object IDs (except client and master); move wallet creation into each test body using createFunded
For each file: recalculate TICKET_COUNT = total wallets across all concurrent tests; add budget comment showing calculation
For each file: verify budget ≤ 99 XRP; if exceeded split into two files each with own beforeAll and faucet call
Set { timeout: 90_000 } on each it.concurrent that creates wallets or submits transactions inside the body
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: These files have 13–18 tests each. Main work: move any wallet/state creation from beforeAll into each test, change it( to it.concurrent(, recalculate budget. If a wallet was shared in beforeAll, each test now calls createFunded for its own copy.

userStories.title: Convert large network test files to it.concurrent
userStories.description: As a developer, I want large network test files converted to it.concurrent with per-test isolation, splitting into multiple files where the faucet budget is exceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: Change every `it(` to `it.concurrent(` in: tests/e2e/channel/channel.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/credential/credential.network.test.ts, tests/e2e/nft/nft.offers.test.ts, tests/e2e/check/check.network.test.ts
For each file: remove all shared sequence/ID/wallet let variables (except client and master); move all object creation inline into each test body
For each file: recalculate TICKET_COUNT for all-concurrent execution; add budget comment
For each file: if budget exceeds 99 XRP, split into file + file2 (e.g. check.network.test.ts + check.network2.test.ts), distributing tests evenly, each with own beforeAll
Set { timeout: 90_000 } on each it.concurrent that funds wallets or submits transactions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: 22–20 tests per file. check.network.test.ts pre-creates check IDs in beforeAll — move inline. channel.network.test.ts pre-creates channel IDs — move inline. Extract IDs from --json output of CLI calls. Budget likely requires splitting at least some of these files.

userStories.title: Refactor escrow.network.test.ts and convert to it.concurrent
userStories.description: As a developer, I want escrow.network.test.ts fully refactored from its shared-wallet/pre-created-sequence pattern to per-test isolation, then converted to it.concurrent.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove all file-level `let` variables except `client` and `master` — specifically delete: sender, receiver, finishTimeSeq1, finishCondSeq, finishJsonSeq, finishNoWaitSeq, finishAccountSeq, cancelBasicSeq, cancelJsonSeq, cancelNoWaitSeq, cancelAccountSeq, listEscrowSeq
Each it.concurrent test creates its own sender and receiver wallets via createFunded inside the test body
Each it.concurrent test that needs an escrow creates it inline via runCLI(['escrow', 'create', ..., '--json']) and parses the sequence from the JSON output

For time-based finish tests: set FinishAfter = now + 15s inside the test body; wait inline with `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 16000))` after submit; safe because all tests wait concurrently in parallel
For condition-based finish tests: create the escrow with --condition inline in the test body

Budget calculation: 23 tests × avg 2 wallets × avg 5 XRP = 230 XRP → exceeds 99; split into escrow.network.test.ts (~10 tests) and escrow.network2.test.ts (~13 tests) each with own beforeAll and faucet call
Each split file has its own TICKET_COUNT with budget comment showing: N tests × W wallets × A XRP + T × 0.2 ≤ 99 ✓
Set { timeout: 90_000 } on all escrow it.concurrent tests (network + optional wait)
All 23 original escrow tests still exist and pass across both files
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is the hardest file. The beforeAll currently creates 9 escrows with shared sequences — each test must instead create its own escrow inline. Time-based finish tests each independently wait 16s, but since they run concurrently the file total wait is still ~16s not 16s×N. Use runCLI with --json to extract sequence numbers from escrow create output.
